content.text:
Looking at the image, the track curves to the right ahead with the car positioned on the left side of the track, and the dashed center line is visible curving rightward.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 93, target = 93 + 5 = 98
- Waypoint 98: x=0.8494, y=2.8385
- Car: x=1.0229, y=2.8993
- Bearing = atan2(2.8385 - 2.8993, 0.8494 - 1.0229) = atan2(-0.0608, -0.1735)
- Both negative → third quadrant: angle = atan2(0.0608, 0.1735) = 19.3° → bearing = -(180° - 19.3°) = -160.7°
- Current heading: -76.35°
- Delta = -160.7° - (-76.35°) = -84.35° → steer RIGHT (sharp)

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Track curves sharply right with delta of -84°, car is left of center heading south-southeast while track continues south-southwest, requiring maximum right steering at reduced speed.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Track curves sharply south near waypoints 91-100 (x decreasing, y decreasing)",
    "Heading must swing from -76° toward -160° to follow track south-southwest",
    "Negative delta means steer right (negative steering_angle)",
    "At delta > 50°, use speed=0.75 and steering=-25°",
    "Car is left of center (is_left_of_center=true) — acceptable for right-curving track",
    "Track width 0.76m — narrow, maintain control through turn",
    "After sharp right turn, waypoints continue south then loop back east",
    "Avoid overcorrecting — once delta < 15°, reduce steering to -5°",
    "Car is progressing through turn section (~79% progress)",
    "all_wheels_on_track=true — car is stable on track"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [0.986, 1.009, 1.018, 1.023],
    "y": [3.096, 3.032, 2.967, 2.899],
    "yaw": [-74.5, -73.4, -74.9, -76.4]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": -84.35,
  "speed": 0.75,
  "steering_angle": -25.0
}
```
